$description: FieldOps Motion Primitives — raw duration and easing values. Do not consume directly. Alias through semantic/motion.tokens.json. Field context: operators move fast, sunlight reduces visual acuity, tasks are time-critical. Motion must be invisible when idle and decisive when it fires.

duration.$description: Duration scale. Every step is a deliberate choice, not a linear ramp. Validated against P50 frame budget on a 60 Hz Android mid-range device (16.67 ms/frame). Nothing here exceeds 1 frame at the fast end, nothing exceeds 18 frames at the slow end.

0.$description: Forced instant — accessibility prefers-reduced-motion target. Maps to instant semantic.

50.$description: Below perception threshold (~40–60 ms). Feels like hardware response, not software.

100.$description: Micro feedback — tap ripple, toggle snap. Matches finger lift latency expectation.

150.$description: Fast UI response — hover states, badge updates.

200.$description: Standard interactive response. Apple HIG 'immediate' zone upper bound.

300.$description: Modal / sheet entry. Material M3 standard enter.

400.$description: Complex layout shift — step advancement, multi-part reveals.

500.$description: Slow narrative — onboarding, completion celebration. Use sparingly.

easing.$description: Easing vocabulary. Named for semantic intent, not visual description. All values are cubic-bezier(x1, y1, x2, y2). CSS-ready. Spring approximations computed via Framer Motion stiffness/damping mapping then sampled to 4-point bezier using spring2bezier methodology.

linear.$description: Raw linear — only for progress fills and continuous loaders. Never for discrete UI transitions.

standard.$description: Workhorse. Asymmetric: fast out, slow settle. Matches Material M3 'Standard' (0.2,0,0,1). Applied to most enter + property transitions where no directional drama is needed.

enter.$description: Elements arriving. Decelerating into rest position — communicates 'landed'. Material M3 Decelerate. Apple uses this for sheet presentation. Mathematically: zero initial velocity, then rapid decel to rest.

exit.$description: Elements leaving. Accelerating out — does not linger. Material M3 Accelerate. Fast leading edge. Crucially: exits should be faster than enters (field operators need information gone, not farewell).

spring-snappy.$description: Spring overshoot approximation. Framer Motion: stiffness=400, damping=28, mass=1. One controlled overshoot — used for step completion checkmark, successful action confirm. NOT for destructive/error states. Peak overshoot ~8% past target.

spring-gentle.$description: Critically-damped spring — no overshoot, organic feel. Framer Motion: stiffness=260, damping=28. For panel slides, sheet entries. Matches Apple UISpringTimingParameters with damping ratio 1.0, response 0.35s. The 'correct' feel for native iOS sheet.

bounce-subtle.$description: Micro bounce — lighter than spring-snappy. Framer Motion: stiffness=300, damping=20. For status badge appearance, icon arrival. ~3% overshoot.

decelerate.$description: Aggressive decel. For elements entering from off-screen (bottom sheet, slide-in panel). Arrives fast, stops precisely.

accelerate.$description: Aggressive accel. Exits only. Sheet dismiss, panel close. The element is 'gone' within the first third of duration.

sharp.$description: Brutal fast snap. For attention-critical state changes: offline → syncing flip, error appearance. No organic feel — this is a system alert, not a transition.
